title: Lophiolepis schelkownikowii
taxonomy: Lophiolepis schelkownikowii is a species of palm tree native to Mexico and Guatemala. It belongs to the family Arecaceae and is classified within the genus Lophiolepis. The genus Lophiolepis is part of the tribe Attaleeae and is closely related to the genus Coccothrinax. This classification suggests that Lophiolepis schelkownikowii shares characteristics with other species within the Arecaceae family. Further research is needed to confirm the exact relationships within the Attaleeae tribe.
benefits: Lophiolepis schelkownikowii, a species of fern, has been found to have various health benefits due to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Its extracts have been studied for their potential in treating certain diseases and conditions. Research suggests that Lophiolepis schelkownikowii may have antimicrobial and hepatoprotective effects. However, more extensive studies are needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption. The plant's potential benefits make it a subject of interest in the field of natural medicine.
